summary: Verified async post-creation architecture end-to-end (backend + frontend). Added new pytest file /app/backend/tests/test_async_posts.py with 6 tests covering: (1) text-only draft auto-finalizes to status='ready' with real telegraph_url, (2) draft with media returns status='uploading', slots=[block indices], telegraph_url=null in both GET /posts/{id} and /posts list, (3) uploading all slots increments media_done and flips status uploading→processing→ready with telegraph_url and cover_url populated, (4) bad slot index and text-block slot both return 400, (5) upload to nonexistent post returns 404, (6) publishing an unfinalized draft returns 400 with Russian detail 'создаётся'. Also fixed one stale regression test (TestCoverUrl.test_telegram_api_has_send_photo_and_is_used) — publish_post was refactored to call helper _do_publish, so the source assertion now targets _do_publish (plus checks publish_post delegates to it). Full backend regression: 50/50 pytest tests pass. Frontend E2E via Playwright: login (admin@mediabot.local/admin123) → Новый пост → filled editor-title/editor-description → uploaded cover PNG via cover-file-input → clicked editor-save → navigated to '/' → upload-jobs panel appeared with upload-job-{id}, upload-job-bar-{id} (blue bar), upload-job-status-{id} (1/1 · Готово) → within 3 seconds the post row transitioned from status 'СОЗДАЁТСЯ'+'создаётся…'+publish-disabled to status 'ГОТОВ'+telegraph link (open-link-{id}=https://telegra.ph/TEST-Async-E2E-…) + publish-enabled. Clicked publish → toast 'Сначала настройте канал' (graceful, no crash, no 500). Dashboard did NOT crash on null telegraph_url (regression bug from previous version fixed). Cleanup verified: 0 TEST_ posts remain.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py _finalize_post: if create_post_page raises, status is set to 'failed' — good — but the media_ids already stored in GridFS become orphan (never cleaned up because delete_post only walks post.media_ids on the doc). Consider retry/cleanup on failed finalization.

server.py upload_media_slot: no size/type guard — a client could POST any file (including HTML or 100 MB video) into a photo slot; watermark.apply_watermark will raise but the error propagates as 500 rather than a friendly 400. Consider validating file.content_type against block.type kind BEFORE reading full file into memory.
server.py upload_media_slot: reads entire file into memory (await file.read()) before uploading — for a 100 MB video this pins the whole thing in RAM. Consider streaming to GridFS/R2 for videos.

server.py _finalize_post: race condition — if two concurrent slot uploads both see media_done >= media_total after their $inc, both call _finalize_post; the second one is guarded only by the status check at the top (must be 'uploading' or 'processing') and the first one sets status='processing' before finalize runs, so the second sees 'processing' too. Recommend using an atomic findOneAndUpdate({status:'uploading'}, {$set:{status:'processing'}}) to gate finalize to exactly one caller.

UploadContext.jsx: startJob does NOT report the finalize/processing phase to the UI — after the last upload completes it flips job.status='done' immediately, but the backend still needs to run _finalize_post which may take a few seconds (Telegraph API call). During that gap the dashboard shows the job as 'Готово' but the post row still says 'СОЗДАЁТСЯ'. Not a bug, minor UX polish: extend jobs UI with a 'processing' phase driven by the polled post status.
Frontend: still using deprecated on_event('startup') (carried over).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (login, editor, async upload progress, status transition uploading→ready, telegraph link, publish graceful 400, delete all verified)

seed_data_creation: All TEST_ posts self-cleanup via try/finally client.delete inside pytest classes and via API cleanup post-E2E. Final state verified: 0 TEST_ posts remain, no channel configured, watermark disabled.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New async architecture: POST /api/posts/draft creates a shell doc with blocks pre-populated (text block if description, then N photo/video blocks with url=null, pending=true). Response {id, slots:[block indices for media]}. If no media slots -> _finalize_post runs inline (auto-ready). POST /api/posts/{id}/media/{idx} uploads one file into that slot ($set url + pending=false, $inc media_done). When media_done reaches media_total AND status=='uploading', status flips to 'processing' and _finalize_post runs (creates Telegraph article, sets cover_url + telegraph_url + status='ready'; if publish_after and channel configured, calls _do_publish and sets published=true). Frontend UploadContext manages a jobs[] state driving upload-jobs progress panel. Dashboard polls /posts every 2.5s while jobs.length>0. All data-testids present: new-post-btn, editor-title, editor-description, cover-file-input, editor-save, upload-jobs, upload-job-{id}, upload-job-bar-{id}, upload-job-status-{id}, post-row-{id}, status-{id}, open-link-{id}, no-link-{id}, publish-{id}, delete-{id}. No mocks used — real Telegraph API + real GridFS.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found in the new async architecture. Previously reported bugs (Dashboard crash on null telegraph_url + missing progress bars) are both fixed and confirmed via E2E: null telegraph_url now shows 'создаётся…' via no-link-{id}, publish button disabled until telegraph_url exists, upload-jobs panel renders and drives progress correctly. Race-condition concern on concurrent slot uploads noted in code review (not a bug in preview single-user testing but worth hardening).
